main_sections.title: National War Memorial
main_sections.poem_text:
In Bharat's heartland, a grand sight to behold,
The National War Memorial, honours brave stories retold.

With respect and grace, it stands with pride,
Each name etched, each stone, each story to confide.

From battle fields to skies, and to oceans wide,
They stand for Bharat, side by side.

The eternal flame, in silence it glows,
Paying homage to their courage that shows.

Their sacrifice, always in our mind,
In blessed memory, forever remind.

With pride and respect, let's always remember,
At the National War Memorial, their sacrifice forever.
main_sections.poem_appreciation_summary: This reverent poem pays tribute to the National War Memorial in New Delhi, established in February 2019 to honour soldiers who gave their lives for Bharat. The poem moves through space and scale — from the heartland where the memorial stands, to battlefields, skies, and oceans — to convey the breadth of sacrifice. The central image is the eternal flame that 'glows' in silence, a symbol of undying memory and homage. The tone is solemn and patriotic, built on repetition of words like 'pride', 'respect', 'memory', and 'sacrifice'. Key devices include apostrophe (addressing readers collectively: 'let's always remember'), anaphora ('Each name etched, each stone, each story'), and personification (the flame glowing 'in silence'). The rhyme scheme (couplets throughout: behold/retold, pride/confide, wide/side, glows/shows, mind/remind, remember/forever) gives the poem a formal, memorial quality appropriate to its subject. This is a recitation poem — the textbook instructs students to read it aloud, recite it with the class, and memorise it.

reading_for_comprehension.section_name: Let us read + Let us discuss + Let us think and reflect

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Let us do these activities: 1. Read the poem aloud. 2. Recite the poem with your classmates and teacher. 3. Remember the poem.

question_bank.stem: Read the poem aloud, then recite it with your classmates and teacher. Memorise the poem.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: 1. Visit the website of the National War Memorial and learn more about it. https://www.mygov.in 2. Plan a visit to the National War Memorial with your teacher/parents.

question_bank.stem: Visit the National War Memorial website (https://www.mygov.in) and find out three interesting facts about the memorial. Share with your class.
Plan a visit to the National War Memorial with your teacher or parents. Write a short note on what you would like to observe and why.
